title: Bridge Essay: Causal models and network instability in neurological and psychiatric disorders:

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Knowledge/Gnosis** (Phase 14) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Knowledge/Gnosis to mechanistic neuroscience through Causal, models, and, network, instability.

**Causal models and network instability in neurological and psychiatric disorders: Computational and clinical perspectives.** — Behav Brain Res. PMID: 41448497. DOI: 10.1016/j.bbr.2025.116007. Self-diagnosis-the capacity of asystem to detect and correct its own failures-is adefining property of adaptive systems. In the brain, recursive self-monitoring underlies perception, action, and cognition, but it is also fragile: small imbalances between priors and prediction errors can destabilize entire networks. Similar recursive instabilities are observed in immune collapse during sepsis and in failure modes of artificial intelligence (AI). Here, we advance aunified framework of causal mo
